summary: Phase A multi-industry CRM backend testing completed. All 17 new tests passed (industries catalog, register with industry, login/me payload shape, switch industry RBAC, funnel terminology, regression on tickets/leads/stats). Previous 19/19 support-ticket regression suite also passes. Total: 36/36.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 2002 lines — recommend splitting into routers/modules (auth, leads, dashboard, organization, support) for maintainability.

Funnel endpoint only maps the last stage label via display_labels; intermediate raw stages ('New','Contacted','Interested') are returned as-is even for non-education industries (e.g. Healthcare would still show 'New'/'Contacted' instead of 'Inquiry'/'Consulted'). This is acceptable for Phase A since underlying lead.status values are still education-specific, but the inconsistency between funnel stages and industry-specific default_pipeline_stages should be addressed in a follow-up.

PUT /api/organization/industry only updates organizations.industry — it does NOT re-seed lead_sources, lead_statuses or pipeline_stages for the new industry, nor migrate existing data. If product expects a 'fresh start' on industry switch, document or implement that. Currently switching from education to it_software keeps the education lead sources unchanged.
register endpoint silently coerces unsupported industry to 'generic' (per spec). Consider logging this so the operator can detect typos in the signup form.

default_lead_statuses and default_pipeline_stages from INDUSTRY_CONFIG are defined but NOT seeded on registration — only default_sources is seeded. Either seed them too or remove from config to avoid dead config.
get_current_user does an extra DB call per request for organizations (industry, name). For high-traffic endpoints consider caching on the JWT or in a short-lived in-memory cache.
test_credentials: Used credentials from /app/memory/test_credentials.md (orgadmin@demo.com / Demo@123, counselor@demo.com / Demo@123, telecaller@demo.com / Demo@123).
seed_data_creation: Tests create temporary orgs/users with emails matching test_*@phaseatests.com and clean them up via direct Mongo session-scoped fixture (before & after). One temporary lead is created in demo org and deleted at end of TestRegression.test_create_lead_still_works.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase A multi-industry is fully green on the backend. test_multi_industry.py is the canonical regression for industry templates. If the main agent re-seeds lead_statuses/pipeline_stages per industry, add seed-verification tests similar to test_register_seeds_default_lead_sources. Note: Pydantic EmailStr rejects reserved TLDs like .test — keep using @phaseatests.com (or another non-reserved domain) for synthetic emails.
rca of the issue: N/A — no defects found in the implementation. Only finding during test authoring: initial use of @phasea.test domain was rejected by Pydantic EmailStr (RFC 2606 reserved TLD). Switched test fixtures to @phaseatests.com and all tests passed.
